violations.text: Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Food was relocated. Guidance document posted on walk-in to be followed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2025
A hose was observed attached to the hand sink. A hand sink must be used for no other purpose other than washing hands. Hose was removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

All foods was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. PIC finished cooking items at 2 hours prior, food was observed at 66-68 degrees. Food was placed on sheet pans to further cool. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
The handle for the walk-in was observed falling off. Handle to be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
